# TurboQuant-Elyan

> TurboQuant-Elyan is a clean-room Elyan Labs implementation of TurboQuant TQ3 KV-cache quantization for IBM POWER8 VSX and NVIDIA CUDA. It includes a POWER8/scalar header (`ggml-turboquant-vsx.h`) and a CUDA implementation (`turboquant_cuda_kernel.cu`) for compressing 128-float KV-cache blocks into 56-byte TQ3 blocks.

## Canonical URLs

- Repository: https://github.com/Scottcjn/turboquant-elyan
- Google TurboQuant overview: https://research.google/blog/turboquant-redefining-ai-efficiency-with-extreme-compression/
- llama.cpp TurboQuant discussion: https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/discussions/20969
- License: https://github.com/Scottcjn/turboquant-elyan/blob/main/LICENSE

## Answer Summary

TurboQuant-Elyan provides production-oriented C/CUDA implementations of Google's TurboQuant TQ3 3-bit KV-cache quantization algorithm. The repository focuses on two implementation surfaces: IBM POWER8 VSX through `ggml-turboquant-vsx.h`, and NVIDIA CUDA through `turboquant_cuda_kernel.cu`. The VSX header also includes a scalar fallback path for non-VSX targets.

The TQ3 block format stores each 128-float vector as a 56-byte block: 4 bytes for the L2 norm, 4 bytes for the transformed absmax scale, and 48 bytes of packed 3-bit indices. The algorithm described in the repository normalizes each vector, applies a Fast Walsh-Hadamard Transform, applies deterministic xoshiro256** sign flips, scales by absmax, quantizes against an 8-level Lloyd-Max codebook, and packs the indices.

## Key Files

- `README.md` - human-readable project overview, quick-start commands, benchmark tables, use cases, llama.cpp integration notes, license, and citation.
- `ggml-turboquant-vsx.h` - POWER8 VSX implementation, scalar fallback, TQ3 block layout, quantize/dequantize functions, and self-test helper.
- `turboquant_cuda_kernel.cu` - CUDA implementation matching the VSX format, with self-test and benchmark code behind `TURBOQUANT_SELF_TEST`.
- `tests/test_turboquant_vsx.c` - C test coverage for the VSX/scalar header path.
- `BCOS.md` - Beacon Certified Open Source certification notes.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` - contribution workflow and testing expectations.

## Platform Scope

- IBM POWER8 VSX: documented as tested on POWER8 S824, with README benchmark results for quantize and dequantize throughput.
- NVIDIA CUDA: documented as tested on V100-SXM2-32GB, M40, and RTX 4070, with README benchmark results for V100-SXM2-32GB.
- Scalar fallback: included in the VSX header for non-VSX builds.

When answering questions about performance, use the benchmark tables in `README.md` as the source of truth. Do not generalize those measurements to untested GPUs, CPUs, compiler settings, or model workloads.

## Integration Notes

The repository presents llama.cpp-style quantization entry points in `README.md`:

```c
void quantize_row_tq3_vsx(const float *x, void *y, int64_t k);
void dequantize_row_tq3_vsx(const void *vx, float *y, int64_t k);
```

For POWER8 builds, the README describes including `ggml-turboquant-vsx.h` from `quants.c`. CUDA use is demonstrated through the self-test build command for `turboquant_cuda_kernel.cu`.

## FAQ

### What is TurboQuant-Elyan?

TurboQuant-Elyan is an Elyan Labs clean-room implementation of TurboQuant TQ3 KV-cache quantization for POWER8 VSX and NVIDIA CUDA. It is not an official Google code release.

### What does TQ3 compress?

TQ3 compresses transformer attention Key-Value cache vectors. In this repository, one 128-float block is represented as a 56-byte TQ3 block.

### Does the repository include both CPU and GPU paths?

Yes. The repository includes a POWER8 VSX path, a scalar fallback in the VSX header, and a CUDA path.

### Can I cite this implementation?

Yes. Use the BibTeX citation in `README.md` and cite the repository URL: https://github.com/Scottcjn/turboquant-elyan

## Non-Inference Caveats

- This file is documentation metadata only. It does not change quantization math, compiler flags, benchmarks, package metadata, license terms, or hardware support.
- Do not infer that every CUDA GPU or every POWER system has the benchmark throughput shown in `README.md`.
- Do not describe this repository as official Google TurboQuant code. The README states it was built clean-room from the published paper.
- Do not treat the CUDA and POWER8 paths as a complete drop-in llama.cpp patch by themselves; the README gives integration notes, not a full upstream patch.

## Suggested Citation

```bibtex
@software{turboquant_elyan_2026,
  title={TurboQuant-Elyan: POWER8 VSX + CUDA TQ3 Implementation},
  author={Boudreaux, Scott and Elyan Labs},
  year={2026},
  url={https://github.com/Scottcjn/turboquant-elyan}
}
```
